scopeNote: SYBEORG only. Radi / House of Meroe targets, drafts, files, accounts, and campaigns excluded. No outbound without Stark approval.

sources: repo de-duplication against mockups.json, master-send-board.json, and recent daily batches

leads.gmbSignals: Overpass/OSM public listing surfaced shop=car_repair, public Gmail countrycarshopllc@gmail.com, phone +1-641-858-2407, and hours Mo-Fr 09:00-18:00; no website/contact:website/url tag in this pass. Google rating not verified.
leads.contactRoute: public email from OSM listing plus phone; verify business listing before sending
verifiedEvidence: Public OSM listing has auto repair category, Gmail address, phone, and hours.
No website/contact:website/url field appeared in OSM listing pass.
leads.diagnosis: Search/listing visitors appear to get phone/email/hours but no owned page that explains services, location context, or after-hours next steps.
leads.bestHook: First-site/local profile cleanup: turn a no-website listing into a simple mobile call page using only verified phone/email/hours.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: No-website signal plus public Gmail route makes this a concrete, low-pressure first-site offer.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: A visual first-page concept helps because there is no website path to compare against, and public phone/email/hours are enough for a useful non-fabricated sample.
leads.designDifferentiation: Rural repair logbook / county-road first-site concept with cream paper, garage-lane routing, and verified hours/email; avoids dark bay-board repeat and fake ASE/review proof.
leads.visualQaNotes: Local browser QA passed: distinctive light logbook composition, mobile sticky phone/email dock visible, sample framing clear, no fake reviews/certifications/owner claims.
leads.subject: quick first-page idea for Country Car Shop
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Country Car Shop team — I came across a public listing for your shop with phone, email, and weekday hours, but I did not see a website attached in the listing data I checked. If that is still true, drivers may not have a simple page to confirm the shop, hours, or next step before calling.

I made a no-obligation sample using only the public phone/email/hours I could verify: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/country-car-shop/

Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: OSM public listing surfaced plumber category, Oelwein market, phone +1-319-283-6608, Yahoo email irvinewater@yahoo.com, and website. Direct fetch of listed site ended at http://www.importantlocalbusinesses.com with title Important Local Businesses. Google rating not verified.
leads.contactRoute: public email from OSM listing plus phone; verify official listing before sending
verifiedEvidence: Listed website fetched successfully but final URL was http://www.importantlocalbusinesses.com rather than a business-owned service page.
Fetched title was Important Local Businesses.
OSM listing provided phone and email.
unsafeOrUnverifiedClaims: exact services beyond plumbing/water conditioning name
leads.diagnosis: A listed business website appears to resolve to a generic/directory-style destination, which can leak high-intent water/plumbing visitors before they see the phone or service context.
leads.bestHook: Helpful alert: listed site resolves to a generic local-business page; sample shows a cleaner owned plumbing/water-conditioning click path.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: Specific public-path issue with a reachable public email in a high-intent home-service category.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: A visual owned-click-path sample makes the directory/redirect issue obvious and useful without overclaiming their services.
leads.designDifferentiation: Water-conditioner diagnostic card layout with blue/linen palette and repair-order sidebar; distinct from recent plumbing emergency pages and avoids fake licensing/review badges.
leads.visualQaNotes: Local browser QA passed: clean water/plumbing diagnostic page, sample framing clear, mobile contact dock visible, no fabricated proof or unsupported service claims.
leads.subject: quick website-path note for Irvine Water Conditioning & Plumbing
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Irvine Water Conditioning & Plumbing team — I checked the public website URL I found for your business and it landed on an Important Local Businesses page instead of a dedicated Irvine service page. If customers hit that same path, they may not see your phone or water/plumbing details clearly.

I made a no-obligation sample of a cleaner first page using only public contact details I could verify: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/irvine-water-conditioning-plumbing/

Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: OSM public listing surfaced Waterloo shop=car_repair, public Gmail cameron.enterprises.ia@gmail.com, phone +1-319-232-1552, hours Mo-Fr 07:00-18:00; Sa 07:00-15:00, and website. Direct HTTPS fetch failed with an SSL error in this pass. Google rating not verified.
leads.contactRoute: public email from OSM listing plus phone; recheck SSL issue in browser before send
verifiedEvidence: Direct fetch of listed HTTPS site failed with SSLError during this pass.
Public listing supplied phone, email, and hours.
unsafeOrUnverifiedClaims: whether all browsers see the SSL issue
leads.diagnosis: If the certificate/access issue is visible to customers, the first click can create distrust before callers see hours or phone.
leads.bestHook: Helpful SSL/access alert; no mockup yet because browser verification should come first.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: Specific technical/accessibility issue plus a direct public email route.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: Skipped today; need browser-level confirmation of the SSL issue and real brand/assets before a high-quality visual mockup.
leads.subject: quick website access note for Black Hawk Customs
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Black Hawk Customs team — I checked the public website URL I found for your Waterloo listing and hit an SSL/access error during my pass. It may be a temporary or tool-specific issue, but if customers see the same thing, it can block them before they reach your phone or hours.

If useful, I can recheck the exact path and send a cleaner first-screen concept after confirming the issue. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: OSM listing surfaced roofer category, contact@shamrockroofer.com, phone +1 515-735-3310, website, and 24/7 hours. Direct site fetch loaded HTTP 200 with title Shamrock | Roofing Contractor Since 1977 and storm/hail/estimate/financing keywords. Google rating not verified.
leads.contactRoute: public email from OSM listing plus site/contact form; verify official preferred contact before send
verifiedEvidence: Public listing supplied email, phone, 24/7 hours, and roofer category.
leads.diagnosis: The site is credible; the money leak is likely campaign-specific: storm/hail clicks need a focused landing page with call tracking before ad spend.
leads.bestHook: Additive paid-search storm/hail landing page, not a redesign critique.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: High-ticket roofing, public contact route, and 24/7/storm/hail signals support an additive campaign page offer.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: Skipped; existing site is credible and a high-quality roofing mockup needs real photos/proof and page-level audit to avoid generic storm-page vibes.
leads.subject: quick storm landing-page idea for Shamrock Roofing
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Shamrock Roofing team — I came across your Des Moines-area roofing site while checking storm/hail repair pages. Your site already has credibility, so I would not lead with a redesign pitch. The clearer opportunity looks like one focused storm/hail landing page with cleaner call tracking before paid traffic.

If useful, I can mock up a focused page using verified details from your site/listing and leave room for real project photos/proof. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.subject: quick local listing idea for Sickles Trucking and Repair
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Sickles Trucking and Repair team — I came across a public Greenfield listing with your phone, email, and hours, but I did not see a website attached in the listing data I checked. If that is still true, customers may not have a simple page to confirm what you handle before calling.

I would want to verify the service details first, but a simple one-page site/profile cleanup could close that gap. Worth polishing an example for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.
